infobox.key: 2009 Saskatchewan New Democratic Party leadership election
infobox.value: was held on June 5–7, 2009, to elect a successor to [Lorne Calvert](/wiki/Lorne_Calvert "Lorne Calvert") as leader of the [Saskatchewan New Democratic Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party"). The election was necessary because Calvert had announced his intention to step down as leader on October 16, 2008. [Dwain Lingenfelter](/wiki/Dwain_Lingenfelter "Dwain Lingenfelter") won on the second ballot, defeating [Ryan Meili](/wiki/Ryan_Meili "Ryan Meili").[[1]](#cite_note-3)
Briere, Karen (2009-06-11). ["Lingenfelter wins NDP leadership"](https://www.producer.com/news/lingenfelter-wins-ndp-leadership/). The Western Producer. Retrieved 2025-03-30.

["Lingenfelter attracting the most NDP leadership cash"](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatchewan/lingenfelter-attracting-the-most-ndp-leadership-cash-1.814900). [CBC News](/wiki/CBC_News "CBC News"). 2009-04-29. Retrieved 2025-03-30.

["Saskatchewan New Democrats choose Dwain Lingenfelter as leader"](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatchewan/saskatchewan-new-democrats-choose-dwain-lingenfelter-as-leader-1.800567). [CBC News](/wiki/CBC_News "CBC News"). 2009-06-06. Retrieved 2025-03-30.

["Deb Higgins"](https://web.archive.org/web/20120428151154/http://saskndp.ca/constituencies/moose-jaw-wakamow). *[Saskatchewan New Democratic Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party")*. Archived from [the original](http://saskndp.ca/constituencies/moose-jaw-wakamow) on 2012-04-28. Retrieved 2025-03-30.
["Higgins runs for NDP leadership"](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatchewan/higgins-runs-for-ndp-leadership-1.842091). [CBC News](/wiki/CBC_News "CBC News"). 2009-01-30. Retrieved 2025-03-30.
["Deputy Premier resigns from politics"](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/deputy-premier-resigns-from-politics-1.205683). [CBC News](/wiki/CBC_News "CBC News"). 2000-07-04. Retrieved 2025-03-30.

["Lingenfelter set to launch NDP leadership bid"](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatchewan/lingenfelter-set-to-launch-ndp-leadership-bid-1.707964). [CBC News](/wiki/CBC_News "CBC News"). 2008-10-28. Retrieved 2025-03-30.

["Saskatoon doctor running for NDP leadership"](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatchewan/saskatoon-doctor-running-for-ndp-leadership-1.857173). [CBC News](/wiki/CBC_News "CBC News"). 2009-02-05. Retrieved 2025-03-30.

["Pedersen to represent Regina South"](https://web.archive.org/web/20071206083849/http://www2.canada.com/reginaleaderpost/news/story.html?id=e630375d-b1ab-4e49-9dac-2f719b3d88a0). *[Regina Leader-Post](/wiki/Regina_Leader-Post "Regina Leader-Post")*. 2007-10-25. Archived from [the original](http://www2.canada.com/reginaleaderpost/news/story.html?id=e630375d-b1ab-4e49-9dac-2f719b3d88a0) on 2007-12-06. Retrieved 2025-03-30.

["Saskatchewan NDP leadership now a race"](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatchewan/saskatchewan-ndp-leadership-now-a-race-1.776858). [CBC News](/wiki/CBC_News "CBC News"). 2009-01-22. Retrieved 2025-03-30.

Previously a member of the Legislative Assembly for [Regina Elphinstone](/wiki/Regina_Elphinstone "Regina Elphinstone") from 1988 to 2000. Won a by-election in [Regina Douglas Park](/wiki/Regina_Douglas_Park "Regina Douglas Park") after the leadership election in September 2009
Later elected to the Legislative Assembly for [Saskatoon Meewasin](/wiki/Saskatoon_Meewasin "Saskatoon Meewasin") in a by-election in March 2017

2009 Saskatchewan New Democratic Party leadership election

2009 Saskatchewan New Democratic Party leadership election

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Date | June 5–7, 2009 |
| Convention | Ipsco Place, [Regina](/wiki/Regina,_Saskatchewan "Regina, Saskatchewan") |
| Resigning leader | [Lorne Calvert](/wiki/Lorne_Calvert "Lorne Calvert") |
| Won by | [Dwain Lingenfelter](/wiki/Dwain_Lingenfelter "Dwain Lingenfelter") |
| Ballots | 2 |
| Candidates | 4 |
| Spending limit | $200,000 |
| [Saskatchewan NDP/CCF leadership elections](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party/Co-operative_Commonwealth_Federation_leadership_elections "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party/Co-operative Commonwealth Federation leadership elections")  1932 · 1936 · 1942 · 1961 · 1970 · 1987 · 2001 · 2009 · [2013](/wiki/2013_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election "2013 Saskatchewan New Democratic Party leadership election") · [2018](/wiki/2018_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election "2018 Saskatchewan New Democratic Party leadership election") · [2022](/wiki/2022_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election "2022 Saskatchewan New Democratic Party leadership election") | |

The **2009 Saskatchewan New Democratic Party leadership election** was held on June 5–7, 2009, to elect a successor to [Lorne Calvert](/wiki/Lorne_Calvert "Lorne Calvert") as leader of the [Saskatchewan New Democratic Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party"). The election was necessary because Calvert had announced his intention to step down as leader on October 16, 2008. [Dwain Lingenfelter](/wiki/Dwain_Lingenfelter "Dwain Lingenfelter") won on the second ballot, defeating [Ryan Meili](/wiki/Ryan_Meili "Ryan Meili").[[1]](#cite_note-3)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2009_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Dwain Lingenfelter was widely perceived to be the frontrunner during the leadership campaign, and attracted the most fundraising.[[2]](#cite_note-4)

Following the first ballot, Yens Pedersen withdrew and Deb Higgins was eliminated. Both chose to endorse Meili.[[3]](#cite_note-5)

## Candidates

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2009_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Candidates")]

### Deb Higgins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2009_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Deb Higgins")]

[Deb Higgins](/wiki/Deb_Higgins "Deb Higgins") was the [MLA](/wiki/Member_of_the_Legislative_Assembly#Canada "Member of the Legislative Assembly") for [Moose Jaw Wakamow](/wiki/Moose_Jaw_Wakamow "Moose Jaw Wakamow"). She was first elected in the [1999 provincial election](/wiki/1999_Saskatchewan_general_election "1999 Saskatchewan general election"). Before entering politics, she was a [union organizer](/wiki/Union_organizer "Union organizer") for [United Food and Commercial Workers](/wiki/United_Food_and_Commercial_Workers "United Food and Commercial Workers").[[4]](#cite_note-6)[[5]](#cite_note-7)

### Dwain Lingenfelter

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2009_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Dwain Lingenfelter")]

[Dwain Lingenfelter](/wiki/Dwain_Lingenfelter "Dwain Lingenfelter") was previously the [MLA](/wiki/Member_of_the_Legislative_Assembly#Canada "Member of the Legislative Assembly") for [Regina Elphinstone](/wiki/Regina_Elphinstone "Regina Elphinstone") from 1988 to 2000 and [Shaunavon](/wiki/Shaunavon_(electoral_district) "Shaunavon (electoral district)") from 1978 to 1986. He had also served as Deputy Premier under [Roy Romanow](/wiki/Roy_Romanow "Roy Romanow"). Before entering politics, he was a farmer.[[6]](#cite_note-8)[[7]](#cite_note-9)

### Ryan Meili

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2009_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Ryan Meili")]

[Ryan Meili](/wiki/Ryan_Meili "Ryan Meili") was a [physician](/wiki/Physician "Physician") from [Saskatoon](/wiki/Saskatoon "Saskatoon").[[8]](#cite_note-10)

### Yens Pedersen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2009_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Yens Pedersen")]

[Yens Pedersen](/wiki/Yens_Pedersen "Yens Pedersen") was a lawyer from [Regina](/wiki/Regina,_Saskatchewan "Regina, Saskatchewan") and the former president of the Saskatchewan New Democratic Party. He was also the Saskatchewan NDP candidate for the constituency of [Regina South](/wiki/Regina_South "Regina South") in the [2007](/wiki/2007_Saskatchewan_general_election "2007 Saskatchewan general election") and [2011](/wiki/2011_Saskatchewan_general_election "2011 Saskatchewan general election") provincial elections.[[9]](#cite_note-11)[[10]](#cite_note-12)

## Ballot results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2009_Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_election&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Ballot results")]

First Ballot

| Candidate | Votes | Percentage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Dwain Lingenfelter | 4,360 | 46.17 |
| Ryan Meili | 2,401 | 25.42 |
| Yens Pedersen | 1,380 | 14.61 |
| Deb Higgins | 1,303 | 13.80 |
| Total | 9,444 | 100.00 |

Second Ballot

| Candidate | Weighted Votes | Percentage | +/- |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Dwain Lingenfelter** | 5,028 | 55.07 | +8.90 |
| Ryan Meili | 4,102 | 44.93 | +19.51 |
| Total | 9,130 | 100.00 |

Won a by-election in [Regina Douglas Park](/wiki/Regina_Douglas_Park "Regina Douglas Park") after the leadership election in September 2009
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Later elected to the Legislative Assembly for [Saskatoon Meewasin](/wiki/Saskatoon_Meewasin "Saskatoon Meewasin") in a by-election in March 2017
